# Maintainer project context

> Auto-generated by Maintainer. Re-run `/maintainer learn` after major code changes.

## Overview

Awesome Intune is a community-curated directory of tools, scripts, and resources for Microsoft Intune professionals. It is a public-facing Next.js web application deployed at awesomeintune.com that lets IT administrators discover, search, and submit tools organized by category (reporting, automation, packaging, troubleshooting, security, configuration, monitoring, migration) and tool type (PowerShell module/script, web app, desktop app, browser extension, CLI tool, API wrapper, documentation). The catalog is stored as individual JSON files in the repository; a set of GitHub Actions workflows automates the entire submission review pipeline from issue creation through security scanning, approval, and subscriber email notification.

## Stack

- **Language:** TypeScript (strict), with plain JavaScript in scripts/ and workflow inline scripts
- **Framework:** Next.js 15 (App Router), React 19
- **Styling:** Tailwind CSS v4, Framer Motion for animations
- **Build tool:** Next.js CLI (`npm run build`); ESLint + TypeScript for static checks (`npm run check`)
- **Test framework:** None (no automated test suite; `@playwright/test` is a dev dependency but no tests are written)
- **Database:** Supabase (PostgreSQL) — stores subscribers, sent_notifications, tool view counts, API keys, and votes
- **Email:** Resend API via `resend` npm package and `@react-email/components`
- **AI/Search:** OpenAI API (GPT-5-nano) for semantic search, description enhancement, and security scanning
- **Bot protection:** Cloudflare Turnstile (`@marsidev/react-turnstile`)
- **Analytics:** Plausible (self-hosted or cloud; accessed via `src/lib/plausible.ts`)
- **Env validation:** `@t3-oss/env-nextjs` + Zod — validation runs at module load time, so `SKIP_ENV_VALIDATION=true` is required in CI when running `npm run check` without secrets

## Directory structure

- `data/tools/` — One JSON file per catalogued tool (~120+ files). `template.json` is skipped by the loader.
- `data/collections/` — Curated collection JSON files (6 collections: app-packaging, autopilot-toolkit, device-management, getting-started, macos-management, troubleshooting).
- `src/app/` — Next.js App Router pages: root homepage, `/tools/[id]`, `/authors/[slug]`, `/collections/[slug]`, `/submit`, `/ideas`, `/best`, `/stats`, `/developers`, and multiple API routes under `/api/`.
- `src/components/` — React components organized by feature: `tools/`, `layout/`, `submit/`, `newsletter/`, `authors/`, `collections/`, `requests/`.
- `src/lib/` — Server and shared utilities: `tools.server.ts` (file-system reads), `tools.ts` (client-safe helpers), `aiSearch.ts`, `supabase.ts`, `resend.ts`, `constants.ts`, `github.ts`, `openai.ts`, `schemas/`, `structured-data.ts`.
- `src/types/` — TypeScript interfaces: `tool.ts`, `collection.ts`, `request.ts`.
- `src/env.js` — Env schema validated with `@t3-oss/env-nextjs`; lists all required server and client env vars.
- `scripts/` — Node.js ESM scripts run in CI or ad-hoc: `capture-screenshots.mjs`, `notify-subscribers.mjs`, `generate-favicons.mjs`.
- `public/screenshots/` — Tool screenshot images organised by tool ID; committed by CI after capture.
- `screenshot-cache.json` — Tracks which tool screenshots have already been captured.
- `.github/workflows/` — All automation (see Existing automations section).
- `.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/` — Two issue templates: `tool-submission.yml`, `tool-idea.yml`.
- `.github/maintainer.yml` — Maintainer bot configuration.
- `next.config.js` — Next.js config; imports `src/env.js`, whitelists GitHub image hostnames, sets security headers.

## Key files

- `src/types/tool.ts` — Canonical `Tool` interface and all related types (`ToolCategory`, `ToolType`, `WorksWithTag`, `SecurityCheck`, `RepoStats`, `Author`). The JSON schema in `data/tools/` must match this.
- `src/lib/tools.server.ts` — Server-only module: reads all JSON tool files from disk, provides `getAllTools()`, `getToolById()`, `getAllAuthors()`, `getAllCollections()`, `getRelatedTools()`, and author/collection helpers used by SSR pages.
- `src/lib/tools.ts` — Client-safe helpers: `filterTools()`, `searchTools()`, `generateAuthorSlug()`. No `fs` usage.
- `src/lib/constants.ts` — `CATEGORY_CONFIG`, `TYPE_CONFIG`, `WORKS_WITH_CONFIG`, `SITE_CONFIG`, `GITHUB_REPO_URL`. Central place for label/color mappings.
- `src/env.js` — All environment variable names and Zod schemas. Add new env vars here first; set `SKIP_ENV_VALIDATION=true` in CI runs that don't need runtime secrets.
- `src/app/page.tsx` — Homepage entry point; calls `getAllTools()` and `getAuthorsForSpotlight()` server-side, renders Hero + ToolsSection + AuthorSpotlight + FAQ.
- `scripts/notify-subscribers.mjs` — Reads `data/tools/`, compares against `sent_notifications` in Supabase, and emails new tools to confirmed subscribers via Resend.
- `.github/workflows/approve-tool-submission.yml` — Most complex workflow; handles the full `/approve` slash command end-to-end.

## Features

- **Tool catalog browsing and filtering** -> `src/components/tools/ToolsSection.tsx` (client-side filtering), `src/lib/tools.server.ts:getAllTools`
- **Semantic/AI-powered search** -> `src/app/api/search/`, `src/lib/aiSearch.ts:shouldUseAiSearch`, `src/lib/openai.ts`
- **Tool detail pages** -> `src/app/tools/[id]/page.tsx`, `src/lib/tools.server.ts:getToolById`
- **Author profile pages** -> `src/app/authors/[slug]/page.tsx`, `src/lib/tools.server.ts:getAllAuthors`
- **Curated collections** -> `src/app/collections/[slug]/page.tsx`, `src/lib/tools.server.ts:getAllCollections`
- **Tool submission form** -> `src/app/submit/page.tsx`, `src/components/submit/`, `src/app/api/submit-tool/`
- **Email newsletter subscriptions** -> `src/app/api/subscribe/`, `src/app/api/unsubscribe/`, `src/components/newsletter/`
- **Screenshot galleries** -> `scripts/capture-screenshots.mjs`, `public/screenshots/{tool-id}/`, `screenshot-cache.json`
- **Tool view tracking** -> `src/app/api/views/`, Supabase `tool_view` table
- **Tool voting** -> `src/app/api/votes/`, Supabase `votes` table
- **Security scan on approval** -> inline script in `.github/workflows/approve-tool-submission.yml` (`performSecurityScan`, `runPatternChecks`, `runAISecurityCheck`)
- **Subscriber email notifications for new tools** -> `scripts/notify-subscribers.mjs`, `.github/workflows/notify-new-tools.yml`
- **Public API (v1)** -> `src/app/api/v1/`, `src/lib/api-auth.ts`
- **Stats page** -> `src/app/stats/page.tsx`, `src/app/api/stats/`
- **Ideas/requests board** -> `src/app/ideas/page.tsx`, `src/app/api/ideas/`, `src/types/request.ts`
- **Best tools page** -> `src/app/best/page.tsx`
- **LLMs.txt endpoint** -> `src/app/llms.txt/`

## Conventions

- **Tool JSON files:** One file per tool in `data/tools/{kebab-case-id}.json`. The `id` field must equal the filename stem. Required fields: `id`, `name`, `description`, `author`, `repoUrl`, `category`, `type`, `dateAdded`. Category and type values are lowercase kebab-case matching the TypeScript union types in `src/types/tool.ts`. Do not add fields not in the `Tool` interface without updating the type.
- **Multi-author tools:** Use the `authors` array field (array of `Author` objects). The top-level `author`, `authorPicture`, `githubUrl`, `linkedinUrl`, `xUrl` fields are legacy single-author fields; `getToolAuthors()` normalises both formats.
- **Security check data:** Written into each tool JSON at approval time and on backfill scans. The `securityCheck` block is machine-managed; do not hand-edit it.
- **Server vs. client modules:** Files in `src/lib/tools.server.ts` use Node `fs` and must only be imported in Server Components or server actions. `src/lib/tools.ts` is safe to import anywhere.
- **Env validation:** `src/env.js` validates at module load. Any `next lint` or `tsc` invocation will fail if required env vars are absent. Workaround: `SKIP_ENV_VALIDATION=true`.
- **Naming:** Page-level components follow Next.js App Router conventions (default export named after page). Component files use PascalCase. Utility functions in `src/lib/` use camelCase.
- **Error handling:** API routes in `src/app/api/` return JSON with `{ error: string }` and appropriate HTTP status codes. No global error middleware.
- **Logging:** `console.log`/`console.error` in scripts and workflow inline scripts. No structured logging library.
- **Adding a new tool manually:** Create `data/tools/{id}.json` matching the `Tool` type, optionally add screenshots under `public/screenshots/{id}/`. No build step required; `getAllTools()` reads files at request time (SSG).
- **Adding a new category or type:** Update the union type in `src/types/tool.ts`, add entry to `CATEGORY_CONFIG` or `TYPE_CONFIG` in `src/lib/constants.ts`, update the dropdown in `.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/tool-submission.yml`, and update normalisation maps in the approve/reject workflow scripts.
- **Collections:** JSON files in `data/collections/` with a `tools` array of tool IDs. Add icon/color mapping to `COLLECTION_ICONS` in `src/lib/constants.ts`.

## Test and verification

There is no automated test suite. Verification is done via:

- `npm run lint` — ESLint with `eslint-config-next` and `typescript-eslint`
- `npm run typecheck` — `tsc --noEmit`
- `npm run check` — runs both lint and typecheck (used as the Maintainer `test_command`)
- `npm run format:check` — Prettier check
- Manual browser testing via `npm run dev`

When running `npm run check` in CI without application secrets, set `SKIP_ENV_VALIDATION=true` as an environment variable (already done in `.github/workflows/maintainer.yml`).

## Existing automations and commands

### `.github/workflows/maintainer.yml`
Runs the Maintainer bot (`ugurkocde/Maintainer@v2`). Triggered on `issues` (opened/reopened/edited), `issue_comment` (created), and weekly on Monday at 09:00 UTC. Sets `SKIP_ENV_VALIDATION=true` so `npm run check` can run without application secrets. Configured by `.github/maintainer.yml` (triage, fix, review, stale, commands). Only `ugurkocde` can trigger `/maintainer` or `@maintainer` slash commands.

### `.github/workflows/approve-tool-submission.yml`
**Trigger:** `issue_comment` created, where the comment body starts with `/approve`, the commenter is `ugurkocde`, and the issue has the `tool-submission` label.

**What it does (end-to-end):**
1. Creates a progress-tracking comment on the issue.
2. Parses the issue body to extract tool fields (name, description, author, repoUrl, category, type, social links).
3. Validates required fields and URL formats.
4. Runs a two-phase security scan against the submitted repository: regex pattern checks (obfuscation, remote execution, credential theft, data exfiltration, malicious patterns, hardcoded secrets) and an AI check via OpenAI GPT-5-nano.
5. If all 6 security checks pass (or `--force` flag is used), creates a tool JSON file in `data/tools/` with AI-enhanced description/keywords, commits it, and pushes.
6. Updates the progress comment with full results, closes the issue, and adds the `tool-added` label.
7. On failure, shows which security check failed and instructs the reviewer to use `/approve --force` to override.

**Supported flags on `/approve`:**
- `/approve -name "Custom Tool Name"` — override the tool name
- `/approve --force` — bypass security check failures and approve anyway

### `.github/workflows/reject-tool-submission.yml`
**Trigger:** `issue_comment` created, comment starts with `/reject`, commenter is `ugurkocde`, issue has `tool-submission` label.

**Command syntax:** `/reject --reason <reason> [--message "custom note"]`

**Valid reasons:** `quality`, `relevance`, `incomplete`, `duplicate`, `unclear`. If the reason is invalid, the workflow posts a usage guide and exits. Otherwise it posts a structured rejection comment with guidance, adds the `rejected` label, and closes the issue.

### `.github/workflows/request-info-submission.yml`
**Trigger:** `issue_comment` created, comment starts with `/info`, commenter is `ugurkocde`, issue has `tool-submission` label.

**Command syntax:** `/info [--message "specific question"]`

Posts a standardised "more information needed" comment with standard questions about use case, Intune relevance, and audience. Adds the `needs-info` label. Leaves the issue open.

### `.github/workflows/auto-assign-issues.yml`
**Trigger:** `issues` opened.

Automatically assigns every new issue to `ugurkocde`. No conditions.

### `.github/workflows/notify-new-tools.yml`
**Trigger:** Scheduled daily at 07:00 UTC (08:00 CET), plus `workflow_dispatch` for manual runs.

Runs `node scripts/notify-subscribers.mjs`. The script reads all tool JSON files, queries Supabase for tool IDs already in `sent_notifications`, emails any new tools to confirmed subscribers via Resend, and records sent notifications.

### `.github/workflows/capture-screenshots.yml`
**Trigger:** Weekly on Sunday at 02:00 UTC, `workflow_dispatch`, and `workflow_run` after "Approve Tool Submission" workflow completes successfully.

Runs `node scripts/capture-screenshots.mjs` using Playwright (Chromium). Captures screenshots of tool websites/repos, stores them under `public/screenshots/{tool-id}/`, updates `screenshot-cache.json` and tool JSON files, then commits and pushes changes with `[skip ci]` in the commit message.

### `.github/workflows/backfill-security-scans.yml`
**Trigger:** `workflow_dispatch` only (manual).

**Inputs:**
- `force_rescan` (boolean, default false) — ignore `lastChecked` date and rescan all tools
- `max_tools` (string, default "0" = all) — cap the number of tools scanned

Iterates all JSON files in `data/tools/`, skips tools with a `securityCheck.lastChecked` within the last 30 days (unless `force_rescan`), runs the same pattern + AI security scan logic as the approve workflow, updates each tool JSON with new `securityCheck` and `repoStats`, and commits the changes.

### Issue Templates

- **`.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/tool-submission.yml`** — Used for new tool submissions. Sets label `tool-submission` automatically. Required fields: Tool Name, Description, Author Name, Repository URL, Category (dropdown), Type (dropdown). Optional: Author URL, Download URL, Website URL, Tags, Additional Information. The `approve-tool-submission.yml` workflow parses the rendered issue body by matching `- **Label:** value` patterns.

- **`.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/tool-idea.yml`** — Used for suggesting tools that don't yet exist. Sets label `tool-idea`. No companion workflow processes these automatically; they are purely for community discussion.

### Third-party bots and integrations

No Dependabot, Renovate, or semantic-release configuration is present. The only bot integration is the Maintainer app (`ugurkocde/Maintainer@v2`) configured via `.github/maintainer.yml` and the `MAINTAINER_APP_ID` / `MAINTAINER_APP_PRIVATE_KEY` secrets.
